ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Fasesky appeared via zoom for Gloria Thomas (petitioner).
Mellisa Hoffman appeared via zoom for Norma Thomas and Jacquelynn Smith (objectors).

Discussion was held regarding the petitioner living in the real property rent free, that she failed to qualify for a loan to buyout the other heirs, and that the partition action was dismissed.

The Court overrules the objection and takes the matter under submission with the intent to overrule objections.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obi. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-29-2025 Continued To Nov-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
John Fasesky appeared by zoom for and with Gloria Thomas (Petitioner) by zoom.
Melissa Hoffman and Kirk Musacchio appeared by zoom for and with Norma Thomas (Petitioner) by zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the $1000.00 beneficiary amount, that they are unable to locate Theodore Thomas's successor in interest, and that they could possibly appoint an elisor to accept the $1000.00 on behalf of the beneficiary.

The Court suggested finding the probate section that allows an elisor and file a joint stipulation or joint statement to the Courts. The matter is continued to November 3, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.


Judge: Josh Fredricks. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Fasesky appeared by zoom for and with Gloria Thomas (Petitioner) by zoom.
Melissa Hoffman appeared by zoom for and with Norma Thomas (Petitioner) by zoom.

The Court stated that at the last calling of the case it urged the parties to use a professional fiduciary, that no petition for appointment for a professional fiduciary was filed, that Gloria's petition is in order and can be acted, that Norma's petition is not in order and that it has reviewed the objections. Mr. Fasesky stated that Gloria denies the content of the declarations and wishes to move forward with her petition, that Gloria will marshal assets, provide notice to all parties, account and will treat all of the Beneficiaries fairly and impartially, and that she is bondable. Ms. Hoffman stated that the Probate Examiner's notes in June indicated that there was nothing wrong with Norma's petition, that she filed a declaration that clarified the issues that were raised in the July notes, and that she re-filed a declaration on August 27th that clarified all of the issues. The parties presented oral argument and echoed the same positions and concerns as they did at the prior hearing. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court grants Gloria's petition with full authority and bond at $158,000.00, overrules the objections and denies Norma's petition. Status on close of estate is set for October 12, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petitions for Letters of Administration are under submission with the Court's intent to proceed as indicated.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Continued To Sep-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; John Fasesky appeared by zoom for and with Gloria Thomas by zoom.
Melissa Hoffman appeared by zoom for and with Norma Thomas by telephone.

Ms. Hoffman stated that Gloria did not file anything objecting to Norma being appointed, that she was never notified that there was anything wrong with her petition, that 6. b. should have been checked, and that there is a glitch with the judicial council form because when you do check it, it does not stay checked. Counsel spoke as to the character of Norma and Gloria, whether or not Gloria is unfit to serve, the issue of Gloria's failure to pay debts and being involved in fraud, the relationship/or lack of relationship between Norma and the Decedent, the Decedent's living condition, etc. The Court raised the issue of having a neutral fiduciary serve as the Administrator. Ms. Hoffman stated that she will need to speak with her Client. Mr. Fasesky stated that he has proposed a neutral party, that Ms. Hoffman dismissed it and that he is willing to have a neutral party appointed. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) are continued one time to September 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a meet and confer about having a neutral fiduciary appointed and for having a petition on file from a neutral fiduciary. If the parties are not able to agree on a neutral fiduciary, the Court will act on the petitions that are before it.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
